openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsyn.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing subscriptionsync.Adapter and SyncStateAdapter interfaces. Manages persistence of SubscriptionBillingSyncState records (upsert, get, invalidate) with full ctx-bound transaction support via entutils.TransactingRepo.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping all DB writes
patterns.description: Every method that touches the DB must call entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-bound transaction is honored rather than falling off to the raw client.
patterns.example: entutils.TransactingRepoWithNoValue(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) error { return tx.db.SubscriptionBillingSyncState.Delete()... })

patterns.name: Config + Validate constructor pattern
patterns.description: Constructor takes a Config struct, calls config.Validate() which accumulates errors with errors.Join, and returns (Interface, error).
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (subscriptionsync.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Tx / WithTx / Self triad
patterns.description: adapter implements TxCreator by providing Tx() (starts a new hijacked tx), WithTx() (rebinds adapter to a given TxDriver), and Self() (returns self for non-tx case). This triad is required for entutils.TransactingRepo to work.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txDb := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txDb.Client()} }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: Compile-time assertion var _ subscriptionsync.SyncStateAdapter = (*adapter)(nil) placed at the top of each implementation file.

patterns.name: UTC normalization on time fields
patterns.description: All time values read from or written to the DB are normalized to UTC via .UTC() or lo.ToPtr(t.UTC()) to prevent timezone drift in stored timestamps.

patterns.name: Upsert with OnConflictColumns
patterns.description: Upsert operations use Ent's OnConflictColumns(...).UpdateXxx() pattern keyed on (subscription_id, namespace) to make UpsertSyncState idempotent.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config, adapter struct, New constructor, and the Tx/WithTx/Self transaction triad. Entry point for all adapter construction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding business logic here; all logic belongs in syncstate.go or equivalent domain files.

key_file_guides.role: Implements SyncStateAdapter: InvalidateSyncState, GetSyncStates, UpsertSyncState. Uses Ent SubscriptionBillingSyncState entity.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting TransactingRepo wrapper — raw tx.db calls outside a TransactingRepo will bypass ctx-bound transactions. Missing UTC() normalization on time fields.

anti_patterns: Calling tx.db directly in a method body without wrapping in entutils.TransactingRepo/TransactingRepoWithNoValue
Storing or returning time values without .UTC() normalization
Adding domain or sync orchestration logic into the adapter (belongs in subscriptionsync service layer)
Skipping the compile-time interface assertion var _ Interface = (*adapter)(nil)

decisions.decision: Adapter is scoped exclusively to SubscriptionBillingSyncState persistence; no subscription or invoice business logic lives here.
decisions.rationale: Separation of concerns: the service layer in subscriptionsync owns orchestration; the adapter owns only data access, keeping it independently testable.

decisions.decision: TransactingRepo is used even for single-statement operations.
decisions.rationale: Callers may already have a transaction in ctx; rebinding ensures atomicity with surrounding operations and prevents partial writes.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new adapter method that writes to DB

openmeter/billing/worker/subscriptionsyn.purpose: Crash-recovery reconciler that periodically re-drives subscription-to-billing sync for subscriptions that may have missed event-driven processing. Iterates active subscriptions in paginated windows and calls SynchronizeSubscription for each eligible entry.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate + named constructor
patterns.description: ReconcilerConfig holds all dependencies; Validate() checks each for nil; NewReconciler calls Validate before constructing and returns (*Reconciler, error).
patterns.example: func NewReconciler(config ReconcilerConfig) (*Reconciler, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Paginated window scan with defaultWindowSize
patterns.description: ListSubscriptions iterates pages of 10,000 subscriptions using pagination.Page{PageNumber, PageSize} until an empty page is returned, preventing unbounded memory usage.
patterns.example: for { subscriptions, err := r.subscriptionService.List(ctx, subscription.ListSubscriptionsInput{Page: pagination.Page{PageNumber: pageIndex, PageSize: defaultWindowSize}}); if len(subscriptions.Items) == 0 { break }; pageIndex++ }

patterns.name: SyncState-gated skipping logic
patterns.description: All.ReconcileSubscription skips subscriptions where SyncState.HasBillables is false or NextSyncAfter is nil or in the future, avoiding unnecessary re-sync when the state machine is quiescent.

patterns.name: Errors.Join for non-fatal per-item errors
patterns.description: All() uses errors.Join to accumulate per-subscription reconciliation errors, logging each but continuing to process remaining subscriptions so one failure does not abort the batch.
patterns.example: outErr = errors.Join(outErr, fmt.Errorf("failed to reconcile subscription: %w", err))

patterns.name: clock.Now() for testable time references
patterns.description: Use clock.Now() instead of time.Now() when computing lookback windows and comparing NextSyncAfter to enable clock injection in tests.

key_file_guides.role: Entire reconciler: config, ListSubscriptions (paginated), ReconcileSubscription (single), All (batch with skip logic). No DB access — delegates to subscriptionsync.Service and subscription.Service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Calling time.Now() directly instead of clock.Now(); adding DB access (route through services); missing deleted-customer guard in ReconcileSubscription.

anti_patterns: Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() — breaks time-based tests
Loading all subscriptions into memory without pagination (defaultWindowSize must be respected)
Aborting the entire batch on a single reconciliation error instead of accumulating with errors.Join
Adding Ent/DB imports — this layer must remain service-interface-only with no direct persistence calls
Removing the deleted-customer guard in ReconcileSubscription without replacing with equivalent behaviour

decisions.decision: Reconciler does not own a DB adapter; it orchestrates exclusively through service interfaces (subscriptionsync.Service, subscription.Service, customer.Service).
decisions.rationale: Keeps the reconciler testable with mocks and decoupled from persistence; transactionality is managed by the service layer.

decisions.decision: Force flag bypasses SyncState-based skipping to support manual or admin-triggered full re-sync without altering stored state.
decisions.rationale: Operational requirement: administrators need to force reconciliation during incident recovery without clearing sync state first.
